title: Date Functions
content:
### Date Functions

SQL provides built-in functions for manipulating and extracting parts of dates and times.

---

### Current Date and Time

| Function | Returns | Database |
|----------|---------|----------|
| NOW() | Current datetime | MySQL, PostgreSQL |
| CURRENT_DATE | Current date | All major databases |
| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | Current timestamp | SQL Standard |
| GETDATE() | Current datetime | SQL Server |
| SYSDATE | Current datetime | Oracle |

### Extracting Date Parts

```sql
-- MySQL
SELECT YEAR(hire_date), MONTH(hire_date), DAY(hire_date)
FROM employees;

-- PostgreSQL
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date) AS hire_year
FROM employees;

-- SQL Server
SELECT DATEPART(YEAR, hire_date) AS hire_year
FROM employees;
```

### Date Arithmetic

```sql
-- Add / subtract days (MySQL)
SELECT order_date,
       DATE_ADD(order_date, INTERVAL 7 DAY) AS delivery_date
FROM orders;

-- Date difference (MySQL)
SELECT DATEDIFF('2024-12-31', '2024-01-01') AS days_diff;

-- PostgreSQL
SELECT order_date + INTERVAL '7 days' AS delivery_date
FROM orders;
```

### Formatting Dates

```sql
-- MySQL
SELECT DATE_FORMAT(hire_date, '%Y-%m-%d') AS formatted_date
FROM employees;

-- PostgreSQL
SELECT TO_CHAR(hire_date, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS formatted_date
FROM employees;

-- SQL Server
SELECT FORMAT(hire_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS formatted_date
FROM employees;
```

> **Key Point:** Date function syntax varies significantly between databases. Always check your database documentation.
codeExample:
SELECT name,
       hire_date,
       DATEDIFF(YEAR, hire_date, GETDATE()) AS years_employed
FROM employees
WHERE hire_date < DATEADD(YEAR, -5, GETDATE());

quiz.question: Which SQL standard function returns the current timestamp?
